title: Matelea trianae
taxonomy: Matelea trianae is classified within the Apocynaceae family, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family includes over 5,000 species of plants, many of which are known for their toxic properties. Matelea trianae is a member of the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which is one of the largest subfamilies within the Apocynaceae family. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and is known for its distinctive flowers and unique growth habits. As a member of the Apocynaceae family, Matelea trianae is closely related to other plants such as milkweed and periwinkle.
